title: Broker Disconnection Behaviour
summary: Defines the expected behaviour of Decifer when the IBKR connection drops mid-session. Covers what happens to new signals, open positions, and pending orders during the reconnection gap, and what triggers manual intervention.

behaviour_during_gap.new_signals: BLOCKED — _on_disconnected() sets dash['ibkr_disconnected']=True; _check_kill() treats this the same as a kill flag, blocking all order submission cleanly.
behaviour_during_gap.open_positions: HELD — no auto-exit on disconnect. Positions remain open in IBKR. IBKR itself holds GTC stops independently.
behaviour_during_gap.pending_orders: LEFT IN PLACE — IBKR holds DAY and GTC orders independently of the bot connection. No cancel-on-disconnect.
behaviour_during_gap.dashboard: dash['status'] set to 'disconnected' or 'reconnecting (N/10)' — visible on dashboard header.
behaviour_during_gap.alert_on_exhaustion: _send_reconnect_exhausted_alert() fires after all 10 reconnect attempts fail.

reconnect_protocol.on_exhaustion: _send_reconnect_exhausted_alert(max_attempts); bot stays alive but ibkr_disconnected=True indefinitely

manual_recovery: If reconnect exhausts, Amit restarts bot.py manually. reconcile_with_ibkr() runs automatically on startup to re-sync in-memory state with IBKR positions.

signal_block_implementation.checked_in: _check_kill() — returns True (blocking) when either killed OR ibkr_disconnected is True
signal_block_implementation.coverage: All 3 downstream order submission guards (agent sell, buy execution, sentinel) call _check_kill() — single check point covers all paths

known_limitations: If IBKR disconnects while a market order is mid-fill, the fill may or may not complete — reconcile_with_ibkr() on restart resolves this.
Pending stop-loss orders in IBKR continue to be active during disconnect (IBKR-side) — positions are protected but the bot cannot modify them until reconnected.
If reconnect exhausts and Amit does not restart the bot, new signals are permanently blocked for that session.
files: orders.py — reconcile_with_ibkr() (called on startup after crash/restart)
